item.title: Meta Bets $200 Billion It Can Show Massive AI Data Center Will Pay Off
item.description: Investors want to see whether the company’s huge facility in rural Louisiana is worth the cost

item.title: Nutrition Care Startup Raises $100 Million to Help GLP-1 Treatment
item.description: Nourish, a nutrition counseling startup, has raised $100 million in a new round of funding to help people make dietary changes while taking GLP-1s, with the goal of boosting and preserving improvements from the weight-loss medications.

item.title: The EU Is Seeking to Ease Trade Strains With US and China
item.description: The world’s three major economic engines are jockeying to reset their trading relationships

item.title: Fashion Giant Loved by Hollywood Stars Crashes in Brazil Markets
item.description: To the outside world, Brazilian fashion giant Azzas 2154 SA appears to be having a breakout moment. Its latest winter campaign featured Sarah Jessica Parker; Meryl Streep turned up to a premiere of The Devil Wears Prada 2 in its black Scarpin heels; and Justin Bieber has been seen sporting its kaleidoscopic puffer jacket.

item.title: Bond Markets Give Yippy Vibes Just as Kevin Warsh Is Set to Become Fed Chair
item.description: A selloff in US Treasuries has taken some yields to their highest in years.

item.title: Ecarx, Backed by Geely’s Li, to Sell Robotaxis to US Startup
item.description: Ecarx Holdings Inc., a company backed by Chinese billionaire Li Shufu, said it reached an agreement to deliver robotaxis to US startup May Mobility Inc., in a deal it valued at about $750 million.

item.title: US Reaches Deep Into Asia for Supply-Chain Sovereignty
item.description: The Beijing summit hasn’t stopped the race for AI dominance

item.title: Bond Market’s Woes Go Far Beyond the Price of Oil
item.description: Demographics, debt, AI also weigh on sovereign bonds

item.title: China’s YMTC Kicks Off IPO Process to Tap Booming Memory Demand
item.description: Yangtze Memory Technologies Co. has begun the process for a long-awaited initial public offering, tapping red-hot demand for the chipmakers at the heart of a global AI development boom.

item.title: The AI Boom Is a Dilemma for Retail Investors
item.description: AI may be a fantastic long-term investment, but today’s frothy atmosphere increases the chances of real pain along the way.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Targets ‘Huge Opportunity’ Despite Iran Shock
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s international co-heads say the turbulent start to the year has done little to deter clients, underscoring Wall Street’s bullish outlook despite events like the Iran war.

item.title: Brazil Sends Cheap Soy Oil Abroad as Biodiesel Plan Stalls
item.description: The world’s top soybean producer Brazil is processing record amounts of the crop, but a delay in the country’s plans to boost local demand through biofuel mandates has resulted in an excess of cheap soy oil supplies being shipped overseas.

item.title: Thai Bank Lending Grows as Firms Borrow to Deal With War Impact
item.description: Thai bank lending returned to growth after almost two years of contraction, as large companies increased borrowing to cushion the impact of the Middle East conflict on their businesses.

item.title: Trafigura-Backed Terrafame Studies Producing Scandium in Finland
item.description: Metals firm Terrafame is studying starting producing scandium, a material used in sectors including defense, from its existing uranium operation in Finland, as Europe seeks to secure supplies of critical minerals.

item.title: Fund Managers Boost Stock Allocations by a Record in BofA Poll
item.description: Fund managers chasing the powerful rally in equities increased their allocations to stocks by the most on record this month, a Bank of America Corp. survey showed.

item.title: New York Homes Most Exposed to Hurricane Risk, Beating Miami
item.description: More than 3 million New York metropolitan-area homes are vulnerable to hurricane winds, the most of any region in the country, according to a report from Cotality, a property analytics firm.

item.title: Crypto Crime Escalates With Kidnappings, Cons and Human Coercion
item.description: After a year of kidnappings, assaults and armed home invasions targeting cryptocurrency holders, the industry is racing to harden its defenses.

item.title: Thailand Rolls Back Welcome Mat for Most Foreign Tourists
item.description: Thailand is rolling back some of its tourism friendly visa policies as the government seeks to crack down on crime and illegal employment involving foreign nationals, risking another setback for an industry that remains central to the economy.

item.title: Barclays Says Robots May Offset 60% of China’s Population Slump
item.description: China’s deployment of humanoid robots could offset as much as 60% of its projected decline in the labor force by 2035, according to Barclays Plc, helping sustain the world’s largest industrial base as the population shrinks at a pace not seen in decades.

item.title: EQT CEO Says New Tech Fund Will Help Europe’s Scale-Up Problem
item.description: A new fund targeting Europe’s most promising startups will help the region create more global champions, according to the head of private equity group EQT AB.

item.title: New ASX CEO Must Drive Right Pace of Change, Outgoing Chief Says
item.description: ASX Ltd.’s incoming chief Anthony Attia will need to strike a balance between accelerating the exchange operator’s market infrastructure development while ensuring its stakeholders are not left behind, according to the firm’s outgoing leader.

item.title: Incoming CEO of Poland’s Zabka Seeks to Build Europe’s 7-Eleven
item.description: Poland’s leading convenience store chain, Zabka Group SA, will prioritize European expansion under its incoming chief executive officer.

item.title: Citi Says 5.5% May Be Next Key Level for 30-Year Treasury Yield
item.description: Bond traders are zeroing in on 5.5% as the new “round number” for 30-year US Treasury yields as inflation concerns ripple across markets, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: Bank of England Mulling Alternative to Stablecoin Holding Limits
item.description: The Bank of England is considering an alternative to its proposed limits on stablecoin holdings after a backlash from the industry.

item.title: Nickel Spikes After Report of Indonesian NPI Production Cut
item.description: Nickel prices spiked following a report of further output cuts in Indonesia, the top producer of the metal.

item.title: Fish Feed Firm BioMar Set For Biggest Copenhagen IPO Since 2018
item.description: Danish conglomerate Schouw & Co. set the price range for its initial public offering of fish feed producer BioMar on Tuesday, in what would be Copenhagen’s largest IPO since 2018.

item.title: Yango Targets 10 New African Markets in $150 Million Push
item.description: Ride-hailing operator Yango Group, plans to invest at least $150 million into African expansion this year, as the Dubai-based platform pushes into new markets.

item.title: India’s 360 ONE Seeks Up to $500 Million for Private Credit Fund
item.description: India’s 360 ONE Asset Management is seeking to raise as much as $500 million for its sixth private credit fund, according to people familiar with the transaction, underscoring sustained investor appetite in the country even as fundraising slows elsewhere.

item.title: Deutsche Bank’s Ozan Tarman and Aditya Singhal on Understanding the Macro Risks
item.description: A live conversation on keeping up with the news cycle.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Traders Keep Up With the News Cycle (Podcast)
item.description: It is hard to have a markets conversation that isn’t out of date within a minute or two. But we think this one, with Ozan Tarman and Aditya Singhal of Deutsche Bank, is basically evergreen. This conversation, recorded at our live show at Wilton’s Music Hall in London, is all about fundamentals: How Tarman, DB’s vice chair of global macro, and Singhal, the firm’s head of EM trading across rates, FX and Credit, make sense of conflicting headlines, whether the rally in tech stocks is to be believed
